title.S: How Much Should a Small Business Website Cost in 2026?

excerpt.S: Website pricing is confusing. Here's an honest breakdown of what you should expect to pay — and what you're actually getting for your money.

content.S:
The Honest Answer: It Depends

We know that's not what you want to hear. But website pricing varies wildly because websites themselves vary wildly. A one-page landing page for a food truck is a fundamentally different product than a full e-commerce store with inventory management.

That said, we can give you realistic ranges based on what the market looks like in 2026 — and help you understand what you're paying for at each tier.

The Three Tiers of Small Business Websites

Tier 1: The Landing Page ($500 – $2,000)

This is a single-page website with your essential business information: who you are, what you do, how to contact you. Think of it as your digital business card.

What you get:

  • One responsive, mobile-friendly page
  • Contact form or booking link
  • Basic SEO setup (title tags, meta descriptions, sitemap)
  • Google Business Profile integration
  • Fast load times

Best for: New businesses, freelancers, contractors, food trucks, pop-up shops, and anyone who just needs a professional online presence.

Tier 2: The Multi-Page Business Site ($2,000 – $8,000)

This is a full website with multiple pages: home, about, services, portfolio/gallery, blog, and contact. It's the standard for established small businesses.

What you get:

  • 5-10 custom-designed pages
  • Blog or news section
  • Photo gallery or portfolio
  • Contact forms with email notifications
  • Advanced SEO and analytics
  • Newsletter signup integration
  • Social media integration

Best for: Restaurants, tour operators, professional services (law, accounting, consulting), salons, and retail businesses.

Tier 3: The Web Application ($8,000 – $25,000+)

This goes beyond a website into custom software: user accounts, dashboards, payment processing, booking systems, admin panels, or API integrations.

What you get:

  • Everything in Tier 2
  • Custom backend functionality
  • User authentication and accounts
  • Database-driven features
  • Payment processing
  • Admin dashboard for content management
  • API integrations (Stripe, Calendly, CRM, etc.)

Best for: Businesses that need custom tools, SaaS products, membership sites, or complex booking/scheduling systems.

What Drives the Price Up?

Several factors can push a project toward the higher end of these ranges:

  • Custom design vs. templates. A fully custom design costs more than adapting a template — but it also gives you a unique identity.
  • Content creation. If you need copywriting, photography, or video production, that adds to the budget.
  • E-commerce. Online stores require product management, inventory, payment processing, and shipping — all of which add complexity.
  • Integrations. Connecting to third-party services (CRMs, booking platforms, payment processors) takes development time.
  • Ongoing maintenance. Some developers include maintenance in their pricing; others charge separately. Make sure you know what's included.

Red Flags to Watch For

The website industry is full of low-quality providers. Watch out for:

  • "$99 websites." You get what you pay for. These are usually cookie-cutter templates with no SEO, poor performance, and zero customization.
  • No portfolio. If a developer can't show you previous work, walk away.
  • Ownership traps. Some builders lock you into their platform. If you leave, you lose your website. Always make sure you own your code and domain.
  • No performance guarantees. A beautiful website that takes 10 seconds to load is worthless.
  • Hidden fees. Monthly hosting fees, plugin licenses, "premium support" charges — get the full cost picture upfront.

The GCOAT Difference

We believe in transparent pricing, clean code, and websites that actually work for your business. Every site we build is:

  • Hand-coded. No bloated page builders or unnecessary plugins.
  • Yours. You own the code, the domain, and the hosting. No lock-in.
  • Fast. Sub-2-second load times on mobile.
  • Accessible. Built to WCAG standards so everyone can use your site.
  • SEO-optimized. Structured for search engines from the first line of code.

We work with businesses of all sizes across the U.S. Virgin Islands and beyond. Whether you need a simple landing page or a full web application, we'll give you an honest quote and deliver exactly what we promise.
